section.title: Introduction
section.para: A stemmer reduces a word form to a shorter root. OpenNLP ships rule-based engines such as Snowball, Porter, and the UniNE light and minimal stemmers through the opennlp.tools.stemmer.Stemmer interface. Stemming is language-specific and lossy: it is useful for search and feature reduction, not as a substitute for lemmatization when a dictionary form is required.

section.title: StemmerFactory
Sharing and caching
section.para:
Some stemmer implementations keep mutable engine state and must not be called from several threads at once. SharingStemmer wraps a StemmerFactory and routes each call to a per-thread delegate, so one instance can be shared safely. CachingStemmer adds a bounded per-thread LRU cache of word-to-stem mappings on top of the same factory pattern. Both wrappers return the same stem as a fresh factory stemmer for the same word:
StemmerFactory factory = new SnowballStemmerFactory(SnowballStemmer.ALGORITHM.ENGLISH);
new SharingStemmer(factory).stem("running"); // "run" new CachingStemmer(factory).stem("running"); // "run"
Thread-safe stemmers such as the shareable SnowballStemmer class and the UniNE light stemmers do not need SharingStemmer. Long-running containers should call clearThreadLocalState() when a pooled thread no longer uses a sharing or caching stemmer.

section.title: Light and minimal UniNE stemmers
section.para: The package opennlp.tools.stemmer.light ports Jacques Savoy's UniNE light and minimal stemming algorithms (the same family Apache Lucene ships in analysis-common). Light stemmers strip a modest set of endings; minimal stemmers strip fewer. Instances are stateless, safe for concurrent use, and each instance is also its own StemmerFactory. Input is expected to be lowercase; the stemmers do not fold case.

Languages with a light stemmer include German, Spanish, Finnish, French, Hungarian, Italian, Norwegian, Portuguese, Russian, and Swedish. Minimal stemmers are available for German, English, Spanish, French, Norwegian, and Swedish. Norwegian variants are selected through NorwegianVariety. LightStemmerUsageExampleTest asserts the behavior shown here.
GermanLightStemmer light = new GermanLightStemmer(); light.stem("häusern"); // "haus"
Stemmer shared = new SharingStemmer(light); shared.stem("häusern"); // "haus"
new GermanMinimalStemmer().stem("vaterhäusern"); // "vaterhauser"
